examples.title: Health Information | National Institutes of Health (NIH)
examples.content: Health Information Health Topics Search all health topics Search mental health heart disease cancer sleep diabetes fitness healthy eating stroke Health Highlights Testicular Cancer Learn about testicular cancer treatment, screening, and statistics. Organ Donation Organ donation is the act of taking healthy organs and tissues from one person and putting them into someone else. See the frequently asked questions about organ donation. Detecting Language Difficulties Many kids start talking later than expected, but most eventually catch up. Learn what you should know if your child has language difficulties that persist. Parkinson’s Disease Find out about the causes, signs, and available treatments for Parkinson’s disease, a neurodegenerative disease thought to be caused by the interaction of genes and environment. Your Healthiest Self: Wellness Toolkits Image Your relationships, your emotions, your surroundings, and other aspects of your life impact your overall health. Find ways to improve your well-being with NIH's wellness toolkits. Learn more NIH Clinical Research Trials and You Image It’s your involvement that helps researchers to ultimately uncover better ways to treat, prevent, diagnose and understand human disease. Learn more SuperAgers show unique cell signatures in the brain March 31, 2026 — Researchers linked neuron creation to exceptional recall and memory in older adults. Scientists identify proteins tied to food tolerance March 31, 2026 — New research enhances our understanding of food tolerance and may lead to new therapies for people with food allergies. Using RNA to treat heart attacks March 31, 2026 — An NIH-supported research team developed a minimally invasive therapy that may help the heart recover from a heart attack. Stay Connected! Sign up to receive the NIH Health Information newsletter. Get email updates twice a month about healthy living and wellness from NIH. Subscribe to the newsletter NIH News in Health Image Check out these popular recent stories from our monthly newsletter, which brings you practical health news and tips based on NIH research: Be A Citizen Scientist Sore, Scratchy Throat? Breaking Down Food Delivering Safer Pregnancies Your Body’s Disease Defenses More stories » Health Information Resources Your Healthiest Self: Wellness Toolkits NIH Clinical Research Trials and You Talking to Your Doctor MedlinePlus Health Info Health Care Providers & Facilities HealthCare.gov Contact Us What health topics would you like to see included on this site? Submit your ideas, feedback, and comments This page last reviewed on March 21, 2025

examples.title: NIH News in Health | A monthly newsletter from the National Institutes of Health, part of the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
examples.content: April 2026 Download printable version (700.95 KB) Detecting Language Difficulties DLD Can Have Subtle Signs Many kids start talking later than expected. Most eventually catch up with other kids their age. But some have language difficulties that persist. This can be a sign of DLD. Protect Against Air Pollution Breathe Better Indoors and Out You can’t see most air pollution. You can’t smell much of it, either. But polluted air—both outdoors and indoors—can still affect your health. Stopping Middle-Age Spread Tired, Achy Eyes? Buffering Childhood Stress
